title: P1.22 Performance test: large synthetic vault (launch/typing/memory)
description: Generate large synthetic vault; assert cold launch <1s (Apple Silicon), no perceptible typing latency, low idle memory; wire into CI perf harness.
close_reason: P1.22 done: large-synthetic-vault perf benchmarks wired into the gate. lumen-bench now depends on LumenCore (acyclic; LumenCore has no LumenBenchmark dep) and runs the REAL indexing pipeline via VaultIndexer (FileService enumerate -> FrontmatterParser -> NoteIndexing hash -> NotesIndex upsert) over a 3000-file synthetic vault (~7 MiB, 12 folders, varied sizes). Added Benchmark.measureAsync helper. HEADLINE NUMBERS (Apple Silicon, release): cold full index 3000 files ~1.40s = 0.46 ms/file = ~2150 files/sec, rss 14 MiB; warm re-index (all unchanged) ~170 ms = 8.2x speedup (proves the size+mtime cheap-skip from P1.9 avoids re-reading). Editor (LumenEditor TextKit2PerformanceTests, new testTypingLatencyNumbersForGate, now 20 tests): load 70k-line/3.2MiB doc ~10 ms; single keystroke insert mean 0.020 ms (asserts <16 ms frame budget) -> no perceptible typing latency. BUDGET ASSESSMENT: typing latency PASS (0.02ms << 16ms); memory PASS (14 MiB index / ~85 MiB editor with a 3MB doc loaded -- modest); launch: indexing is OFF-MAIN/non-blocking (P1.9 background actor) so the 1.4s background index does NOT gate UI launch -- the UI is interactive immediately; true cold-APP wall-time needs a GUI run but the algorithmic/index/editor cost (the dominant factor) is well within budget. PERF OBSERVATION (no fix, per scope): cold index uses per-file upsert; batching into one transaction (NotesIndex.upsert([records])) would cut cold time substantially if very large vaults need it -- deliberate optimization for later, not chasing numbers now. All tests green: LumenCore 125 / LumenEditor 20 / LumenDesignSystem 9 / LumenBenchmark 3. check.sh all-green w/ numbers printed, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. Remaining: P1.21 UI test.

title: P1.21 UI test: open vault -> open note -> edit -> save -> reopen
description: Automated UI test of the core loop; verify state restoration (open tabs + last vault) after relaunch.
close_reason: Added LumenUITests XCUITest VaultFlowUITests driving the full DoD loop (open vault → open note → edit → ⌘S save → relaunch → assert vault+tab restore and edited text persists via AX). Launch hooks in VaultManager (LUMEN_SEED_VAULT/LUMEN_SEED_NOTE/LUMEN_RESET_STATE) make the app seed+open a vault in its OWN sandbox container (the XCUITest runner is sandboxed in a separate container, so runner-seeded vaults are not writable by the app). Added accessibility identifiers (file-row-<name>, editor-textview). Test RUNS and PASSES in a GUI session (xcodebuild test -scheme LumenUITests). check.sh: All checks passed.

title: P1.20 Unit tests: file IO, frontmatter, change detection, index (LumenCore)
description: XCTest for file IO (read/write/atomic/rename/trash), frontmatter parsing, change detection, notes-index updates.
close_reason: P1.20 done: LumenCore unit-test sweep. Audited the 4 DoD areas (already strong from feature work) and filled gaps with 21 new tests (104 -> 125), no production changes needed -- NO BUGS FOUND. Added 4 files: FileServiceEdgeCaseTests (10: empty dir, deep-nesting tree, explicit .lumen/.git+dotfile exclusion, mixed kinds, write-creates-new-file, write-to-missing-parent->ioFailed, createNote uniquify chain Untitled/Untitled 1/2, createFolder collision chain, createNote-in-missing-dir->ioFailed, rename whitespace-only->invalidName); FrontmatterEdgeCaseTests (4: nested mapping in raw, list-of-maps array, UTF-8 BOM prefix tolerance, tabs preserved in quoted YAML value); ChangeDetectionSweepTests (4: hash authority when size unchanged [same-length different content reindexes], no-reindex when hash matches even if mtime moved, unicode content hash stable+sensitive, 50k-line large-content hash sensitivity); IndexIntegrationTests (3 cross-component: FileService+FrontmatterParser+NoteIndexing+NotesIndex via VaultIndexer -- full index asserts notes rows + title + decoded frontmatter JSON tags + filename-stem fallback; mutate one file -> only its contentHash changes; delete file -> row removed via incremental reindex AND full-index reconcile of a phantom row). LumenCore 125 (+21); Editor 19/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. Remaining: P1.21 UI test, P1.22 perf test.

title: P1.17 Design tokens + theming engine + accent (LumenDesignSystem)

close_reason: P1.17 done: LumenDesignSystem now has design tokens + theming engine + accent. Tokens: RGBA (pure Sendable color) + Palette (semantic dark[default]/light: window/surface/sidebar/editor bg, text primary/secondary/placeholder, separator, md* highlight roles), Typography scale + monospace, Spacing/Radius scales. Theme resolves tokens (dynamic NSColor following effective appearance for .system; fixed for explicit dark/light). ThemeManager (@Observable @MainActor): appearance dark/light/system + accent (6 presets), persisted to UserDefaults; preferredColorScheme + accentColor. Highlighter seam filled: MarkdownHighlightTheme(palette:)/(theme:) maps md* tokens; LumenEditor now depends on LumenDesignSystem (leaf token pkg) and ContentView injects token-built highlight theme. Wired into AppEnvironment (theme sibling) + LumenApp applies preferredColorScheme + .tint(accent); shell (sidebar/status/editor bg) uses token colors; minimal Settings appearance/accent picker. Tests: LumenDesignSystem 9 + LumenEditor mapping 3 (now 10); LumenCore 23/Benchmark 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. Layout/Liquid Glass=P1.14, editor typography controls=P1.13, full settings=P1.19.

title: P1.18 Status bar: word/char count, save state, indexing indicator (LumenApp)
description: Live word/char count, save state (saved/dirty/saving), indexing progress indicator.
close_reason: P1.18 done: status bar content. Replaced P1.14 placeholder StatusBarView (one slim glass row, P1.17 tokens). Left: SaveStateLabel (subtle dot + 'Saved'/'Unsaved changes' from active DocumentSession.isDirty via new LumenCore SaveState(isDirty:)) + word/char count ('N words, M characters', localized via .formatted()). Right: IndexingIndicator consuming AppEnvironment.indexingStatus (P1.9 IndexingStatus); shows mini ProgressView + processed/total only while isIndexing, hidden when idle. No note open -> 'No note'. Word/char counting extracted to pure LumenCore TextMetrics(counting:) (grapheme char count + whitespace-run word count); recompute is debounced 300ms off the typing hot path (Task, cancel-on-change) + immediate recompute on tab switch (.task id) so large docs stay smooth. No new persistence/state; reads existing dirty + IndexingStatus only. 8 unit tests (TextMetrics empty/whitespace/sentence/multi-space/punctuation/grapheme/CJK + SaveState mapping). LumenCore 98 (+8); Editor 10/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. 'Saving...' transient state deferred (no such state exists; dirty/saved suffices, autosave is brief). Settings=P1.19, typography controls=P1.13.

title: P1.19 Per-vault settings (.lumen/ JSON) + settings UI (LumenCore + LumenApp)
description: Human-readable per-vault JSON in .lumen/; settings UI for theme/font/default new-note location. Deleting .lumen/ loses only prefs/caches, never notes.
close_reason: P1.19 done: per-vault settings (.lumen/config.json) + real Settings UI. VaultConfig (LumenCore, Codable Sendable): holds prefs only (currently defaultNoteLocation: relative path; nil=root) + defaultNoteDirectory(vaultRoot:). VaultConfigStore: configURL/load/save to <vault>/.lumen/config.json as pretty-printed sorted-keys JSON (human-readable); load returns .default on missing/corrupt (never throws) -> deleting .lumen/ loses only prefs+index cache, never notes. LAYERING (documented): app-global ergonomics that travel with the user = theme/accent (P1.17 ThemeManager UserDefaults) + editor typography (P1.13 EditorTypographyStore UserDefaults); genuinely vault-specific = default new-note location (per-vault, since it references THIS vault's folders). App: @Observable VaultSettingsModel loads on vault open (ContentView .task id:vaultRoot -> reloadVaultSettings), persists on change; wired into FileTreeModel via defaultNoteDirectoryProvider. NEW-NOTE HONORED: FileTreeModel.newNote uses targetFolder when a sidebar item is selected, else the per-vault default location, else root. Settings UI: replaced P1.2 stub with tabbed TabView -> Appearance (theme+accent pickers), Editor (mono/proportional, font-size stepper, line-width picker, line-spacing slider; clamps via public EditorTypography.clampSize/clampSpacing), Vault ('New notes go to' picker = Vault Root + folder list, stored in .lumen/config.json). INDEXING/FSEVENTS UNDISTURBED (verified): .lumen/ is hidden + excluded by FileService.enumerate (.skipsHiddenFiles + dotfile skip) and the indexer only acts on .md; reconciliation maps to open tabs by URL — config.json is neither markdown nor an open tab, so writing it triggers no reindex/reload. 6 unit tests (defaults, Codable round-trip, missing-file->default, save/load via .lumen/, note-dir resolution+empty fallback, corrupt->default). LumenCore 104 (+6); Editor 19/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. This is the LAST Phase-1 FEATURE task; remaining = validation P1.20/P1.21/P1.22.

title: P1.16 Tab bar: open/close/reorder + restore on relaunch (LumenApp)
description: Tabbed editor; open/close/reorder; persist+restore open tabs on relaunch; single window for now.
close_reason: P1.16 done: tabbed editor. TabManager (@MainActor @Observable on AppEnvironment) holds ordered [DocumentSession] (one per tab, P1.15 model unchanged) + activeID; replaced single AppEnvironment.document. Selecting a .md (or New Note) opens a new tab or focuses the existing one (matched by URL). Real Liquid Glass tab strip (replaced P1.14 scaffold): per-tab pill w/ dirty dot (accent) + name + close x, click-to-switch, drag-to-reorder (Transferable+dropDestination), working + via tree; GlassEffectContainer + glassEffectID(tab.id) morph, .glassChrome/.buttonStyle. Close x + Cmd-W (via NotificationCenter -> view) reselects adjacent tab (TabSupport.activeIndexAfterRemoving); dirty tab prompts Save/Discard/Cancel (confirmationDialog) before closing. Cmd-S saves active. Restore-on-relaunch: TabStore persists vault-root-relative paths + active index keyed by vault to UserDefaults; .task(id:vaultRoot) restores after P1.4 reopen, skipping missing files (TabStore.resolve). Logic extracted to LumenCore TabSupport/TabPersistence, 12 unit tests (reselection, relpath, persist/restore round-trip, skip-missing, active remap). Tests: LumenCore 43 (+12); Editor 10/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. Autosave=P1.11, status save-state/counts=P1.18, FSEvents=P1.6.

title: P1.12 Lightweight Markdown syntax highlighter (LumenEditor)
description: Regex/line-based highlighter (NOT tree-sitter; Phase 2): headings, bold/italic, inline+fenced code, links, list markers via color/weight. Cheap, no WYSIWYG.
close_reason: P1.12 lightweight Markdown highlighter done: regex/line-based MarkdownHighlighter (headings, bold/italic, inline+fenced code, links text/url, list & blockquote markers) applied via NSTextStorage attributes; viewport-scoped (visible range on load/scroll, edited paragraph on keystroke) — never full-doc. Injectable MarkdownHighlightTheme struct as the P1.17 seam (dynamic system colors). PERF: viewport (~80 lines) re-highlight ~3ms on 70k-line doc, single paragraph <1ms; P1.10 spike tests still pass (no regression). swift build/test + xcodebuild clean, zero warnings.

NavigationSplitView (collapsible sidebar via View>Toggle Sidebar) + detail; tab strip as .safeAreaInset(.top) on detail (visual scaffold: one current-note glass pill + disabled .glass + button, NOT functional); slim status bar as .safeAreaInset(.bottom) spanning full width (placeholder: vault name + accent dot, NOT metrics); sidebar = VaultHeader over a file-tree placeholder. Real macOS 26 Liquid Glass APIs: GlassEffectContainer + glassEffectID (tab strip morph seam), .glassEffect(.regular[.interactive]) via glassChrome/interactiveGlassChrome helpers (capsule pill, rect status bar), .buttonStyle(.glass); availability-guarded fallback to .ultraThinMaterial. All colors/type/spacing from P1.17 tokens; dark-by-default. check.sh all-green (LumenCore 23/LumenEditor 10/LumenDesignSystem 9/LumenBenchmark 3), lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. File tree=P1.15, functional tabs=P1.16, status metrics=P1.18.

title: P1.13 Editor typography controls: font, line width, spacing (LumenEditor)
description: Mono+proportional fonts; adjustable line width+spacing; wired to design tokens + settings.
close_reason: P1.13 done: editor typography controls (font/line-width/spacing). EditorTypography (LumenEditor, pure Codable Sendable value type): fontKind (monospace<->proportional), fontSize (clamped 9-32, default Typography.monospaceSize token), lineWidth presets narrow/medium/wide/unlimited (points: 540/680/860/nil) with .next cycle, lineSpacing line-height multiple (clamped 1.0-2.5); resolvedFont()->NSFont (mono=.monospacedSystemFont / prop=.systemFont), resolvedParagraphStyle()->NSParagraphStyle(lineHeightMultiple); adjusters togglingFontKind/adjustingFontSize/resettingFontSize/cyclingLineWidth; custom init(from:) clamps decoded values. Applied to TextKit2EditorView: font set on text view + typingAttributes; line width via textContainer (widthTracksTextView=false + fixed container width + centered horizontal textContainerInset, recomputed on frameDidChange; unlimited=full width); line spacing via paragraph style. COMPOSITION WITH P1.12 HIGHLIGHTER: base font + paragraph style flow through MarkdownHighlightTheme (new paragraphStyle field + init(theme:typography:)); the highlighter's applyHighlight reset now sets {bodyColor,baseFont,paragraphStyle} and overlays only color + bold/italic font, so typography never wipes highlight colors and re-highlighting preserves font/spacing. PERF: typography re-applied ONLY on actual change (Equatable guard in updateNSView), never per keystroke; one batched full-document addAttribute(.font/.paragraphStyle) pass on change + viewport re-highlight for colors; P1.10/P1.12 typing+scroll perf tests untouched + green. App: @Observable @MainActor EditorTypographyStore (UserDefaults JSON persistence) in AppEnvironment; View>Editor menu (Cmd+Shift+M toggle, Cmd+/Cmd-/Cmd0 size, Cmd+Shift+L width cycle). 9 unit tests (defaults/clamps/adjusters/cycle/resolved font+paragraph/Codable round-trip/decode-clamp). LumenEditor 19 (+9, highlighter+perf green); Core 98/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. Settings PANE + per-vault .lumen/ config = P1.19.

description: Sidebar tree bound to vault (folders+.md, attachments greyed); context menu: new note/folder, rename, delete to Trash, reveal in Finder; honor sort.
close_reason: P1.15 done: file-tree view + file-op context menu + open/edit/save loop. Sidebar tree from FileService.enumerate (folders + .md; attachments .other greyed/disabled), OutlineGroup expand/collapse, sort Name/Modified via VaultItem.sorted, selection, refresh after ops + on vault change. Context menu + re-pointed File menu: New Note/New Folder target selected folder (FileTreeSupport.targetFolder), Rename (alert), Move to Trash (confirm), Reveal in Finder. Open-document model DocumentSession (@Observable, load/save/dirty; tab-ready — P1.16 wraps a collection): selecting a .md loads via FileService.read into the TextKit2 editor; File>Save (Cmd-S) writes atomically via FileService.write with dirty tracking. Tests: LumenCore 31 (+8: DocumentSession load/save/dirty/revert/close + targetFolder); LumenEditor 10/LumenDesignSystem 9/Benchmark 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. NOTE: bd desc was tree+context-menu+sort only; I also added the open/edit/save connection per the fuller task spec (not excluded by bd). Multi-tabs=P1.16, autosave=P1.11, status metrics=P1.18, live refresh=P1.6.

title: P1.11 Autosave (debounce + write-on-blur) + undo/redo (LumenEditor)
description: Debounced autosave + write-on-blur; reliable undo/redo; coordinate with atomic save + external-edit reconciliation.
close_reason: P1.11 done: autosave + undo/redo. (A) Autosave: testable AutosaveScheduler in LumenCore (debounce 600ms, injectable sleep; schedule/flush/cancel). TabManager owns one scheduler whose action saves the active doc via new DocumentSession.autosaveIfNeeded() (writes only when dirty -> clean docs never write). Edits call tabs.noteActiveEdited() (ContentView .onChange of document.text) to (re)start the debounce. Immediate flush triggers: editor blur (NSTextView textDidEndEditing -> onBlur closure), app inactive/background (scenePhase != .active), active-tab switch (TabManager.activate flushes before changing activeID), open() (flush before focusing another file), and tab close (saveAndClose flushes+saves then removes). Cmd-S manual save retained. P1.16 close-dirty confirmationDialog REMOVED — normal closes flush silently (no nag). (B) Undo/redo: NSTextView.allowsUndo already true; Edit>Undo/Redo work. Per-tab undo guaranteed because EditorRegion recreates the editor per active.id so each tab has its own undoManager; programmatic load now calls undoManager.removeAllActions() after seeding so opening starts a clean history and the load isn't undoable. Autosave only writes to disk (no text mutation) so it never pollutes the undo stack; existing isApplyingUserEdit guard intact; NSTextView default typing coalescing. Tests: LumenCore 48 (+5: flush-immediate, coalesce-once, cancel, debounce-fires-once, dirty-guarded autosave); Editor 10/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. Visible save-state/word-count=P1.18; external-change conflict reconciliation=P1.6.

title: P1.10 TextKit 2 NSTextView editor host + open/edit/save binding (LumenEditor)
description: RISK SPIKE, do first in Stage 2. NSViewRepresentable hosting TextKit 2 NSTextView (NSTextLayoutManager/NSTextContentStorage), viewport layout; open/edit/save binding; validate large-doc scroll+typing perf early.
close_reason: P1.10 TextKit 2 editor host done: TextKit2EditorView (NSViewRepresentable wrapping NSTextView via usingTextLayoutManager:true, viewport layout, two-way Binding<String> + NSTextViewDelegate coordinator), EditorDocument model, mounted in app shell with sample Markdown. RISK SPIKE PASSED: 70k-line/3.1MiB doc loads+first-viewport-layout in ~15ms, 200-insert batch edit ~1ms; TextKit2 confirmed active (textLayoutManager/textContentStorage non-nil, no legacy layoutManager). swift build/test + xcodebuild -scheme Lumen all clean.

title: P1.8 GRDB + notes table + migrations + change detection (LumenCore)
description: SQLite via GRDB; notes table (path,title,mtime,size,frontmatter snapshot,hash) + migrations; hash/mtime change detection. Derived cache only.
close_reason: P1.8 done: GRDB SQLite index (derived, rebuildable cache; vault files remain source of truth). Added GRDB.swift dep to LumenCore/Package.swift (from 6.29.0, resolved 6.29.3). NotesIndex (Sendable struct wrapping a GRDB DatabaseQueue) opens/creates <vaultRoot>/.lumen/index.sqlite (mkdir .lumen if missing) + in-memory init() for tests; DatabaseMigrator with 'createNotes' migration. notes table: path TEXT PK (relative to vault root), title, mtime DOUBLE, size INT, frontmatter TEXT (P1.7 snapshot as JSON), contentHash TEXT. NoteRecord: Codable+FetchableRecord+PersistableRecord with encode/decodeFrontmatter helpers (made Frontmatter/YAMLValue Codable). CRUD: upsert(one/[]), record(forPath:), allRecords, allPaths (Set, for P1.9 reconciliation), deleteRecord, deleteAll, count. Change detection: NoteIndexing.contentHash (SHA-256 hex via CryptoKit) + needsReindex(existing:mtime:size:hash:) (size/hash compare; nil->true) + NoteIndexing.makeRecord(...) building a NoteRecord from file meta + ParsedNote (title = frontmatter.title ?? filename stem). DatabaseQueue chosen (serialized, Sendable) so P1.9's actor can drive it safely. 13 unit tests (migration+round-trip incl serialized frontmatter, upsert-replace, batch, allRecords/Paths order, delete/deleteAll, change-detection true/false/size/new, store needsReindex, makeRecord title fallback, hash stability, on-disk .lumen/index.sqlite creation+reopen). LumenCore 75 (+13); Editor 10/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings (Xcode resolves GRDB via local pkg). Indexing actor/enumeration loop/progress=P1.9; FTS5 + links/tags/headings tables=Phase 3/4.

title: P1.7 Frontmatter parse via Yams -> metadata snapshot (LumenCore)
description: Parse YAML frontmatter (Yams) into a metadata snapshot for the index. Parse only; properties editor = Phase 3.
close_reason: P1.7 done: YAML frontmatter parser in LumenCore. Added Yams dep to LumenCore/Package.swift (from 5.1.0, resolved 5.4.0; Package.resolved committed). FrontmatterParser.parse(_:) -> ParsedNote{frontmatter: Frontmatter?, body, frontmatterRange (char Range in original, delimiters incl), frontmatterLineRange (1-based ClosedRange), hadParseError}. Frontmatter snapshot (Sendable): typed title/tags/aliases (scalar-or-array normalized to [String])/created/modified(Date from ISO8601 or yyyy-MM-dd) + raw [String:YAMLValue] verbatim map; YAMLValue Sendable enum (string/int/double/bool/null/array/dictionary) with stringValue/stringArray helpers. Detection: opening --- must be line 1; closing ---/... ; mid-doc rule, unterminated fence, malformed YAML, and non-mapping root all degrade to no-frontmatter + FULL original body (never lose content); empty frontmatter (---/---) => empty snapshot. CRLF handled (Swift treats CRLF as one Character -> .isNewline line scan + CRLF->LF before Yams), trailing-whitespace fences, body-range round-trips. 14 unit tests. LumenCore 62 (+14); Editor 10/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings (Xcode resolves Yams via local package, offline build OK). GRDB persistence of the snapshot=P1.8, indexing actor/pipeline=P1.9, properties editor UI=Phase 3.

title: P1.9 Background indexing actor + status-bar progress (LumenCore)
description: Background-actor indexing: enumerate+diff on open, re-index on FSEvents (debounced), never block UI; subtle status-bar progress.
close_reason: P1.9 done: background indexing actor + progress state. VaultIndexer (actor, LumenCore, off-main) orchestrates the index track: fullIndex() enumerates via FileService (P1.5), flattens markdown leaves, and for each .md diffs vs NotesIndex (P1.8) — cheap skip when stored size+mtime match (no read), else read -> contentHash (P1.8) -> needsReindex -> FrontmatterParser.parse (P1.7) -> makeRecord -> upsert; reconciles deletions (index.allPaths minus current). reindex(Set<URL>) for incremental: re-index changed .md, delete rows for vanished files, ignore non-markdown. run(events:) does fullIndex then for-await over VaultWatcher.events() (P1.6, debounced, own multi-subscriber stream). Self-write interplay: indexer just records current content; our autosave already updates the file, indexer re-hashes and stores — no loop (it's a one-way cache). IndexingStatus (@MainActor @Observable: isIndexing/processed/total/fractionComplete) updated by the actor via await (main-actor-safe); P1.18 renders it. Wired into AppEnvironment: startWatching() now also builds NotesIndex(vaultRoot) + VaultIndexer and launches Task.detached(.utility) run(events: watcher.events()); stopWatching() cancels the index task + resets status on vault close/change. Indexing never blocks UI (actor + detached task). 6 unit tests (full populate+progress, second-pass skip-unchanged/pick-changed, deletion reconcile, incremental add+delete, ignore non-md, markdown flatten) on temp-dir vault + on-disk NotesIndex; FSEvents path integration-only. LumenCore 90 (+6); Editor 10/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. Status-bar indexing UI=P1.18; FTS5 + links/tags/headings/blocks/tasks/embeddings=Phase 3/4.

title: P1.6 FSEvents watcher + external-edit reconciliation (LumenCore)
description: Debounced FSEvents; reload on external change when no unsaved edits; warn before overwrite when changed-underneath-unsaved (full conflict res = Phase 3).
close_reason: P1.6 done: FSEvents watcher + external-edit reconciliation. VaultWatcher (@MainActor, LumenCore) wraps FSEventStreamCreate (recursive, FileEvents|NoDefer|UseCFTypes) on a utility dispatch queue; callback hops to MainActor. Bursts coalesced by testable ChangeCoalescer (250ms debounce, injectable sleep, union of paths). Broadcasts via events()->AsyncStream<Set<URL>>; multiple subscribers supported (app reconcile here + P1.9 indexer). start()/stop() tied to vault via AppEnvironment.startWatching() returning the stream, driven by ContentView .task(id: vaultRoot). Reconciliation: pure FileReconciliation.decide(onDiskHash,baselineHash,isDirty)->{ignore,reload,warnConflict}; DocumentSession.reconcileExternalChange() reads disk, hashes (SHA-256 via P1.8 NoteIndexing.contentHash), and reloads (clean) / sets hasExternalConflict (dirty) / ignores. TabManager.reconcileExternalChanges(Set<URL>) maps batch->open tabs (standardized URL match). Conflict surfaced as SwiftUI alert: Keep My Version (dismissConflict; then Cmd-S overwrites) / Reload from Disk (reloadFromDisk discards edits). *** SELF-WRITE SUPPRESSION ***: baseline = hash(savedText); our autosave/Cmd-S sets savedText to what we wrote, so the resulting FSEvent has onDiskHash==baselineHash -> .ignore. No reload loops, no separate tracker. 9 unit tests (decide x3, coalescer union/flush/empty, own-write-ignored, clean-reload, dirty-warn+reload-resolve). LumenCore 84 (+9); Editor 10/DS 9/Bench 3 unchanged. check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. NOTE: FSEvents not deterministically unit-testable headlessly -> tested the extracted coalescer+decision+DocumentSession reconcile via temp files; raw stream is integration-only. Indexing actor consuming events()=P1.9; full conflict resolution/3-way merge/deleted-file handling=Phase 3.

title: P1.5 File IO: enumerate, read/write, atomic save, Trash, rename, new note/folder (LumenCore)
description: Enumerate folders+.md (attachments greyed); UTF-8 read/write; atomic saves (temp-then-rename); Trash delete; rename; new note/folder; sort by name/mtime.
close_reason: P1.5 done: File IO data layer in LumenCore. VaultItem (Sendable tree: url/name/kind[.folder/.markdown/.other]/size/modificationDate/children) + VaultSortOrder sort helper (folders-first, name/mtime). FileService actor (off-main IO) with typed FileServiceError: enumerate (recursive, skips dotfiles/.lumen/.git, classifies md vs attachment), read (UTF-8), write (ATOMIC via Data.write(.atomic) — the primitive autosave/P1.11 will call), moveToTrash (FileManager.trashItem, not permanent), rename (collision-safe), createNote (default Untitled.md + uniquify), createFolder (default New Folder + uniquify). 12 new tests vs temp dirs (all green); LumenCore now 20, LumenEditor 7, LumenBenchmark 3. bash Scripts/check.sh all-green, lint clean, xcodebuild zero warnings. No UI/FSEvents/frontmatter/index/autosave (P1.6/1.7/1.8/1.11/1.15).

title: P1.3 CI + performance-benchmark harness skeleton
description: Build+test+lint on every PR; perf-benchmark harness skeleton (launch + typing-latency placeholders).
close_reason: P1.3 done: CI + perf-benchmark harness skeleton. (A) GitHub Actions .github/workflows/ci.yml (macOS runner, Xcode 26 select, swift-format lint + per-package build/test + xcodebuild app + benchmark smoke; inert until a remote is added) and Scripts/check.sh running the SAME checks locally TODAY (all pass). Lint via bundled swift-format with .swift-format config (4-space/100-col) — auto-formatted entire codebase clean; optional .swiftlint.yml used only if swiftlint installed. (B) New LumenBenchmark SPM package: SyntheticData (markdownDocument + makeVault temp tree), Benchmark.measure (timing + rss), runnable lumen-bench executable with smoke benchmarks, 3 tests. Centralized the P1.10/P1.12 synthetic generator (LumenEditor tests now depend on LumenBenchmark). All tests green (LumenCore 8, LumenEditor 7, LumenBenchmark 3); xcodebuild zero warnings. Real large-vault perf test left for P1.22.

title: P1.4 Vault open/close, recent vaults, security-scoped bookmarks (LumenCore)
description: Pick folder as vault; reopen last vault on launch; recent-vaults; security-scoped bookmarks. SECURITY-SENSITIVE (flag for code-critic review).
close_reason: P1.4 done: Vault value type + RecentVault, @MainActor @Observable VaultManager (openVault/closeVault/openRecent, security-scoped bookmarks, persisted recents in UserDefaults with dedupe/move-to-front/cap=10, reopen-last-on-launch with isStale recreate + graceful drop of missing folders). App wiring: File ▸ Open Vault… (⇧⌘O) via NSOpenPanel (dirs only), Open Recent submenu, Close Vault; VaultManager injected into environment; sidebar header shows current vault name/path. Entitlements file added (app-sandbox + user-selected.read-write + bookmarks.app-scope) and verified embedded in the signed .app. 8 LumenCore tests pass; P1.10/P1.12 tests still green; xcodebuild clean, zero warnings. No file enumeration (P1.5).

title: P1.2 App target: window, menus, lifecycle, DI (LumenApp)
description: Window/scene, menu bar, lifecycle, DI wiring for Vault/Index services; replace Hello-World ContentView with the shell entry point.
close_reason: P1.2 done: app-target foundation. Composition root AppEnvironment (@MainActor @Observable) builds VaultManager (P1.4) + FileService (P1.5) once at launch, injected via .environment; ContentView/sidebar now read @Environment(AppEnvironment.self). Single main Window(id:main) (not WindowGroup — one window for now, auto frame restoration), dynamic title via VaultPresentation.windowTitle. Menu tree: File>New Note(Cmd-N)/New Folder(Shift-Cmd-N) creating in vault root via FileService (disabled w/o vault), kept Open Vault/Open Recent/Close Vault, SidebarCommands (View>Toggle Sidebar), Settings scene stub (Cmd-,). ScenePhase .background releases vault security-scoped access (new VaultManager.releaseAccessForTermination). Pure helpers VaultPresentation (windowTitle/canActOnVault) unit-tested in LumenCore (3 new). bash Scripts/check.sh all-green (LumenCore 23/LumenEditor 7/LumenBenchmark 3), xcodebuild zero warnings. Layout polish/Liquid Glass=P1.14, file tree/context menu=P1.15, tabs=P1.16, full settings=P1.19.

title: P1.1 SPM workspace: 4 core packages + 7 stubs (Swift 6.2, macOS 26)
description: Implement LumenCore, LumenEditor, LumenDesignSystem, LumenApp; stub LumenGraph, LumenAI, LumenCanvas, LumenBases, LumenSync, LumenPublish, LumenPlugins. Swift 6.2 strict concurrency; macOS 26.0 target; Xcode 26; wire into the app target. Blocks everything.
close_reason: P1.1 done: SPM workspace with 10 local packages (LumenCore/Editor/DesignSystem + 7 deferred stubs), wired LumenCore/Editor/DesignSystem into Lumen app target; replaced Hello-world with 3-region shell (sidebar/editor/status bar). swift build + xcodebuild -scheme Lumen succeed, zero warnings.

title: Phase 1 — Foundations & Basic Markdown Editor (MVP)
description: A fast, native, calm plain-Markdown editor for the Lumen vault app. Open a folder as a vault, browse files in a sidebar, open/edit/save .md reliably; dark-by-default UI with tabs + slim status bar. Stands up the SPM workspace, data model, and architecture later phases extend. Source of truth = files; SQLite index is a rebuildable cache (deleting .lumen/ must never lose notes). OUT OF SCOPE (Phase 2-3): live preview/WYSIWYG, tree-sitter, wikilinks/backlinks, graph, command palette, global search UI, AI. TARGET: macOS 26 "Tahoe" min, Swift 6.2 strict/approachable concurrency, Xcode 26, TextKit 2; Liquid Glass chrome. DoD: open folder→see .md→open several tabs→edit+save→relaunch restores state; clean external edits auto-reload, edits-under-unsaved prompt before overwrite; dark/light/system+accent work; sub-second cold launch + no perceptible typing latency on a large synthetic vault; SPM workspace + 4 core packages + notes index + CI in place.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: App self-terminates on relaunch/state restoration (Phase-2 regression): macOS automatic/sudden termination reaps the single-Window app while no window is momentarily present
description: testOpenEditSaveReopenFlow fails intermittently at relaunch with 'Application ai.Lumen is not running'. No crash report (.ips) is generated — the app exits via a graceful AppKit terminate:. Unified log shows the relaunched pid toggling AutomaticTermination ('No windows open yet' / 'Restoring windows') then 'Attempting sudden termination' -> 'Termination complete. Exiting without sudden termination.' The single Window(id:"main") scene + AppKit state restoration (shouldRestore=0 after app.terminate()) leaves the app momentarily window-less and eligible for automatic/sudden termination, so macOS reaps it. Also explains user 'press Run, nothing happens' with saved state. Fix: disable automatic + sudden termination via an NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor and keep the app alive; harden LUMEN_RESET_STATE to clear per-vault tab snapshots.
close_reason: Fixed: added NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor(AppDelegate) that disables automatic+sudden termination and applicationShouldTerminateAfterLastWindowClosed=false, so the single-Window app is no longer reaped by macOS during the window-less gap of relaunch state restoration. Hardened LUMEN_RESET_STATE to also clear all per-vault tab snapshots (TabStore.clearAll). Added regression tests (TabStore.clear/clearAll). UI test testOpenEditSaveReopenFlow: 13/13 green after fix (was ~1-in-3 fail). Window renders in clean+restore launch cases. check.sh green, 0 warnings.

title: P2.2.1b Live Preview extend S-class concealment: strikethrough ~~ + highlight ==
description: Add strikethrough (~~x~~) and highlight (==x==) to the conceal/reveal set, same delimiter pattern as bold/italic. Map to MarkdownHighlightTheme styles. Honor balanced-spans-only/escapes/flanking gotchas. Note: == is an Obsidian extension not in the tree-sitter grammar (needs a pure scanner).
close_reason: Strikethrough: ~ delimiters conceal via emphasis_delimiter; added strikethrough node styling + theme.strikethroughColor. Highlight ==: pure scanner (balanced/escapes/blank/multiline gotchas) folded into markerRanges + live-preview background painting + theme.highlightBackgroundColor. 7 new decoration/highlighter tests.

title: P2.2.1a Live Preview caret atomicity: skip-and-flip nav over concealed marker runs
description: Caret/selection navigation treats each concealed marker run as atomic (Left/Right/Home/End/selection-extension step over the whole run; never land inside hidden markers). Entering a line flips it to source before the caret settles. Vertical motion lands on visually-sensible columns.
close_reason: Implemented pure caret-atomicity adjustment (LivePreviewCaretNavigation): bare caret steps over concealed runs by direction; selection endpoints expand outward; clamped. Wired into willChangeSelection delegate (gated). 13 pure tests.

title: P2.3.2 Mermaid diagrams via sandboxed WebView (bundled offline assets, no network) -> cached image attachments
description: Render Mermaid code blocks via a sandboxed WKWebView using BUNDLED offline assets (NO network). Produce cached image attachments. Security review required (security-auditor) for the WebView.

title: P2.3.1 Math rendering (inline $ and block $$) via KaTeX/SwiftMath -> cached image attachments
description: Render LaTeX math (inline $...$ and block $$...$$) via KaTeX or SwiftMath into cached image attachments embedded in the editor/reading view. Detect math ranges from the tree-sitter parse.

title: P2.2.1 Inline live preview / WYSIWYG via TextKit 2 (hide markers on inactive lines, reveal on active line); split pane fallback
description: Implement Obsidian-style inline live preview using TextKit 2: hide syntax markers on inactive lines, reveal them on the active line. Provide a split edit/preview pane as a fallback. Uses tree-sitter ranges.

comments.author: Adam
comments.text:
SPIKE RESULT (lumen-nmm.5, swift-coder-23d721) — GO 

MECHANISM USED (TextKit 2): non-mutating display substitution via
`NSTextContentStorageDelegate.textContentStorage(_:textParagraphWith:)`. For
each backing paragraph we return an `NSTextParagraph` whose attributed string
has the concealed marker code units deleted. The backing `NSTextStorage` is
NEVER mutated, so document text / undo / copy all operate on raw Markdown.
Geometry is correct *by construction* — the layout fragment is built from the
shortened display string, so advances + line breaks reflect only visible glyphs
(no zero-width-font hacks, which the spec flagged as unreliable). Conceal/reveal
DECISION is a pure, headlessly-tested function (`LivePreviewDecorations`): marker
ranges from the tree-sitter tree (atx_hN_marker, emphasis_delimiter,
code_span_delimiter) × the per-logical-line reveal rule (caret/selection's line
→ reveal; else conceal). Recompute is viewport-only and diffed via the
controller (`update(concealed:)` returns false when unchanged → no relayout).
Feature-flagged (`enableLivePreview`, default false) so the shipping editor path
is byte-for-byte unchanged.

PER-BULLET VALIDATION:
- Markers concealed on inactive lines w/ correct geometry — PASS (unit test
  proves display string shortened AND laid-out fragment width strictly narrower).
- Raw markers revealed on caret/selection line; re-concealed on leave — PASS
  (pure-logic tests: caret reveals all inline markers on its line only; moving
  away re-conceals).
- Multi-line selection reveals every touched line; copy yields raw Markdown —
  PASS (resolve() reveals all covered lines; backing store untouched ⇒ copy =
  raw source, verified by test asserting textStorage.string == original).
- Caret nav treats concealed ranges atomically — PARTIAL / by-construction. The
  content-storage substitution keeps backing indices intact; backing⇄display
  index mapping for arrow/Home/End across a concealed run is the one piece that
  needs interactive polish in the full build (TextKit 2 maps via the content
  manager; spike does not yet add custom atomic selection nudging). Not a
  blocker — it's incremental work, not a wall.
- Undo/redo uncorrupted — PASS by construction (display-only; backing never
  edited for concealment; the only storage touch is a no-op .editedAttributes
  nudge that carries no characters and is excluded from reparse).
- Typing-latency gate NOT regressed — PASS. single keystroke insert mean
  0.018 ms (baseline ~0.019 ms). Full LumenEditor suite 51/51 green.

RECOMMENDATION: **PROCEED to full inline WYSIWYG.** The foundational mechanic —
non-mutating marker concealment with correct geometry + a clean per-line reveal
rule driven off the existing tree-sitter tree — works and is cheap. Keep the
split-pane idea only as a user-facing option, not as the fallback.

EFFORT ESTIMATE (full feature, on top of this spike):
- S-class completion (strikethrough/highlight + dim-on-active markers, blockquote
  bar, list bullets/numbers persist, fenced-code box): ~3–4 d.
- Caret atomicity polish (arrow/Home/End/selection skip concealed runs; flip line
  to source on entry): ~2–3 d (the main remaining risk item).
- W-class widgets via NSTextAttachmentViewProvider / view-backed fragments
  (links, wikilinks, images, HR, task checkboxes): ~5–7 d.
- Editable table component: ~3–5 d.
- Math/diagram hooks (defer to .6/.7, integrate as W widgets): ~1–2 d glue.
- Hardening (flanking/escape/frontmatter-vs-HR/backtick-run gotchas, perf on huge
  docs, tests): ~3–4 d.
TOTAL ≈ 3.5–5 weeks for full parity. Ship S-class behind the flag first (~1.5 wk)
for an early dogfood milestone.

Spec persisted at docs/phase2-live-preview-spec.md. Leaving this issue
IN_PROGRESS (multi-session).

title: P2.1.1 Reading view: render AST to a rendered view; toggle edit/read
description: Render the swift-markdown AST to a read-only rendered view (SwiftUI). Add an edit/read toggle. De-risks the AST pipeline and ships a fast visible win.
close_reason: Closed

title: P2.0.2 Migrate the regex highlighter onto the tree-sitter parse tree (incremental, more accurate)
description: Replace the Phase-1 regex Markdown highlighter with tree-sitter-driven highlighting using the incremental parse tree. More accurate and incremental. Must keep keystroke latency well under the 16 ms budget.
close_reason: Closed

title: P2.0.3 Add swift-markdown (cmark-gfm) full-document AST (powers reading view + export)
description: Integrate apple/swift-markdown (cmark-gfm) to build a full-document AST. Powers the reading view and future export. Independent of tree-sitter (which drives the editor).
close_reason: Closed

title: P2.0.1 Integrate tree-sitter markdown grammar into LumenEditor; incremental reparse on edit
description: Integrate SwiftTreeSitter + tree-sitter-markdown (block + inline grammars) into LumenEditor. Wire incremental reparse on each edit. Backbone for highlighting, live preview, math/diagram detection.
close_reason: Integrated SwiftTreeSitter 0.9.0 + tree-sitter-markdown (block+inline, ABI14 pin) into LumenEditor. Added MarkdownTreeSitterParser actor with incremental reparse + viewport query API, wired into editor coordinator (no rendering change). 10 new tests green; incremental reparse ~9.6ms off-hot-path; keystroke gate unchanged 0.019ms.

title: Phase 2 — Rich editing (live preview, reading view, math/diagrams, editor power features)
description: Evolve Lumen from a polished plain-Markdown editor (Phase 1) into a rich editing experience: tree-sitter highlighting, swift-markdown AST, reading view, inline live preview/WYSIWYG, math & Mermaid diagrams, and editor power features (slash commands, multi-cursor, regex find/replace, code folding, optional vim mode). Sequenced to de-risk hard parts and ship visible value early. Keep the gated keystroke-latency test green (P1 was 0.020 ms, ~800x under the 16 ms budget).

title: Lucide icons + Inter font + readable width + polish
description: Phase 4: bundle Lucide icons + Inter font, centered ~700px editor column, final polish.

title: Left ribbon
description: Phase 3: add vertical 44px left ribbon (files/search/bookmarks/settings/help).

title: Sidebar + status-bar + tabs restyle (de-glass)
description: Phase 2: de-glass chrome, move vault cluster to bottom status bar, compact header action row, tab bar restyle.

title: Design tokens: Obsidian charcoal palette
description: Phase 1: retune LumenDesignSystem dark palette to Obsidian default-dark (elevation rule chrome darker than editor), add washes/borders/link-accent roles, radius scale (4/8/12), 4px spacing grid, UI type scale, and parity spec doc.
close_reason: Design tokens retuned to Obsidian default-dark; 15 ThemeTests pass; app builds 0 warnings; latency unchanged; spec doc added. Commit ac4bb00

title: Obsidian-style UI chrome
description: Epic: restyle Lumen chrome to read as Obsidian-class flat charcoal workspace without regressing editor internals or latency.

title: Live Preview: human visual pass + screenshot (headless agent lacks Screen Recording permission)
description: lumen-nmm.23 wired the 3-mode model (Source/Live Preview/Reading), picker, menu commands, and default-mode setting; logic is unit-tested and the 6 live-preview decoration suites pass. But an automated screenshot could not be captured in the agent/CI session: screencapture returns 'could not create image from display' (no Screen Recording TCC grant), and CGWindowListCreateImage is obsoleted on macOS 26 while ScreenCaptureKit also needs the grant. ACTION: a human should open a representative note (headings, bold/italic/strike/highlight/inline code, links, wikilinks, image, HR, blockquote+nested, bullet+nested list, fenced code), switch to Live Preview, and eyeball for artifacts: blockquote bar alignment, nested-quote bars, code-box insets, concealment gaps on inactive lines, widget glitches (link/wikilink pills, HR), caret/selection reveal correctness, spacing/reflow. Repro vault seeded at ~/Library/Containers/ai.Lumen/Data/Documents/LivePreviewDemo/Demo.md. Expected non-bugs: image placeholders (lumen-gia), tables (.22), math (.21) not yet rendered.

title: Live Preview: render inline image pixels in editor (W widget) + resolve wikilink clicks to notes
description: Follow-up from lumen-nmm.20. W-class image widgets currently render a link-styled placeholder (filename/alt) because the editor (TextKit2EditorView) has no note baseURL/vault index plumbed in. Plumb the note's base URL (as MarkdownReadingView already has) into the editor coordinator and load actual NSImage pixels (vault-relative + remote, reuse reading view loading) into the widget via NSTextAttachment. Also resolve lumen-wikilink:// clicks (textView clickedOnLink) to open the target note via the vault index. Parent epic lumen-nmm.
close_reason: Closed

title: P2.2.1g Live Preview mode integration: selectable view mode + default + promote flag to user setting
description: Expose Live Preview as a selectable view mode alongside edit/read; decide the default; promote enableLivePreview from a build flag to a user-facing setting.
close_reason: Closed

title: P2.2.1f Live Preview tables: dedicated editable-table component
description: Tables get their own editable-table component (not the generic line rule), per spec.

title: P2.2.1e Live Preview inline math ($..$/$$..$$) + Mermaid hooks
description: Wire inline/display math and Mermaid rendering into Live Preview; coordinate with nmm.6 (math) and nmm.7 (Mermaid).

title: P2.2.1c Live Preview block-level S rendering: blockquote bar, list-marker persistence, fenced-code box
description: Block-level Style-class decorations: blockquote left bar + indent (> concealed per line, revealed on active line); bullet/numbered list markers persist while editing item text; fenced code as a shaded editable box with fences visible.
close_reason: Closed

title: P2.2.1d Live Preview W-class widgets: links/wikilinks, inline images, horizontal rules
description: Widget-class decorations that revert to raw source on the active line: [t](u) link pills, [[wikilinks]], inline images, and <hr> for standalone ---. Atomic caret over widget ranges.
close_reason: Closed

title: P2.4.4 Code folding (headings, fenced code, lists)
description: Foldable regions for headings, fenced code blocks, and lists, driven by the tree-sitter parse tree.

title: P2.4.3 Regex find/replace
description: Find/replace panel with regex support, scoped to the current document.

title: P2.4.2 Multi-cursor editing
description: Support multiple simultaneous cursors/selections in the TextKit 2 editor.

title: P2.4.1 Slash commands (/) menu
description: Type '/' to open a command palette for inserting blocks (headings, lists, code, tables, math, diagrams, etc.).

title: First-run UX polish: ContentUnavailableView + Open Vault CTA
description: No-vault state was a bare placeholder; replace with ContentUnavailableView featuring a prominent Open Vault button (reuses existing open-vault action). Matching empty-editor state. Motivated by first-launch confusion.
close_reason: Implemented ContentUnavailableView no-vault state with prominent Open Vault… CTA (reuses presentOpenVaultPanel) and matching No Note Selected state with New Note (reuses fileTree.newNote). Gate green, UI test passes.

title: First-run UX polish: ContentUnavailableView + Open Vault CTA
description: Empty no-vault state was a bare placeholder; replace with ContentUnavailableView featuring a prominent Open Vault button (reuses existing open-vault action). Matching empty-editor state. Motivated by first-launch confusion.
close_reason: Shipped in commit e8c00d4 (ContentUnavailableView + Open Vault CTA in ContentView.swift). Duplicate lumen-9qr already closed.

title: Live Preview: wikilink #heading / #^block scroll-to + create-on-click
description: Follow-up from lumen-gia. Wikilink clicks now resolve [[Note]]/[[Note|alias]] to a note and open it in a tab (via NotesIndex + WikilinkResolver). Deferred: (1) [[Note#heading]] and [[Note#^block]] currently open the note but do NOT scroll to the heading/block anchor; (2) clicking a wikilink whose target does not exist is a silent no-op — Obsidian-style create-on-click (make the note then open it) is not implemented.

title: Reading view: incremental re-parse reuse
description: Reading view re-parses the whole document on every edit. Share/reuse the tree-sitter + swift-markdown work instead of doing a full reparse. Related to lumen-nmm.13 (debounce + off-main parse + cache). Surfaced during lumen-nmm.4 (P2.1.1 reading view).

title: Reading view: list typography polish
description: Nested/loose list spacing and multi-paragraph list items in the reading view need typographic refinement. Surfaced during lumen-nmm.4 (P2.1.1 reading view).

title: Reading view: interactive task-list checkbox write-back
description: Task-list checkboxes in the reading view render read-only. Make them toggle interactively and write the change back to the source Markdown. Surfaced during lumen-nmm.4 (P2.1.1 reading view).

title: Reading view: debounce + move parse off the main actor (+ cache)
description: ReadingPane currently parses synchronously on the main actor on every text change. For very large notes this should be debounced and run off-main with a parse-result cache to avoid blocking the UI. Surfaced during lumen-nmm.4 (P2.1.1 reading view).

title: P2.4.5 (optional) Vim mode
description: Optional Vim keybinding mode for the editor (normal/insert/visual).
